description: Fix `opentabs start --show-config` failing with 'Port already in use' when the server is already running. The README tells users to run this command to see MCP client config blocks again, but it tries to bind the port before printing config — so it errors out if the server is running. The --show-config flag should print the config and exit without attempting to start the server.

userStories.title: Make `opentabs start --show-config` work when server is already running
userStories.description: As a user who wants to see my MCP client configuration again, I want `opentabs start --show-config` to print the config blocks and exit even when the server is already running, so that I can follow the documented workflow without hitting an error.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs start --show-config` prints the MCP client config blocks (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, OpenCode) with the auth secret pre-filled, then exits with code 0 — regardless of whether the server is running
The command does NOT attempt to bind the port or start the server when `--show-config` is passed
The output format matches the first-time setup config block output from `opentabs start`
If the auth secret is missing (no auth.json), it prints a helpful error telling the user to run `opentabs start` first
`opentabs start` (without --show-config) continues to work as before, including the port-in-use check
`opentabs start --show-config --port 3000` respects the custom port in the printed config URLs
userStories.notes:
File: `platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts`

The `handleStart` function (line ~240) currently runs the port-in-use check at lines 260-268 BEFORE the --show-config logic at lines 287-299. When --show-config is set and the port is occupied, the process exits at line 266 before ever reaching the config printing.

Fix approach: Add an early return path at the top of `handleStart` (after resolving port and config/auth, but BEFORE the port-in-use check) when `options.showConfig` is true. This path should:
1. Read the auth secret from auth.json (using the existing `autoInitialize` or a lighter helper)
2. Print the MCP client config blocks using the existing `printMcpClientConfig` or equivalent helper that's called at lines 287-299
3. Exit with code 0

The auto-initialization logic (creating config dir, generating auth secret, installing extension) should NOT run in the --show-config early path — those are only needed for actual server starts. If auth.json doesn't exist, print an error like 'No auth secret found. Run opentabs start to initialize.'

The README at lines 66-67 documents this command as `opentabs start --show-config  # print config blocks and exit`, so the fix aligns with the documented behavior.
